'''Greedo the Elder''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] who was a member of the [[Tetsu Clan]]. He later became a famous bounty hunter, sired two sons, and became a leader of his clan. He was murdered by his arch rival, [[Navik/Legends|Navik the Red]].

==Biography==
Greedo grew up on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]],<ref name="NEGC" /> but later lived on [[Rodia/Legends|Rodia]], becoming a successful [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]], marrying and siring two sons. As an adult, Greedo began to look upon the traditional Rodian hunts as foolishness, and despite his feared reputation, Greedo refused to join in the gladiatorial combat that his people engaged in.<ref name="Tales" />

After a successful career hunting criminals and outlaws, Greedo came to a violent end when his first-born son was young, dying in the purges of [[Navik/Legends|Navik the Red]].<ref name="Tales" />

Greedo's pregnant wife [[Neela]], along with his son and [[Greedo/Legends|namesake]], and brothers-in-law [[Nok (Rodian)|Nok]] and [[Teeku]], fled Rodia. Greedo's son sought to emulate his father by claiming the bounty on [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], but died as violently as the first Greedo had.<ref name="Tales" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Greedoelderdeath.jpg|thumb|200px|The death of Greedo the Elder.]]

The original intention was that the Greedo scuffling with Anakin Skywalker in the scene that was [[Content cut from Star Wars#Episode I The Phantom Menace|deleted scene]] from [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''The Phantom Menace'']]<ref name="TPM">{{Film|I}} {{C|[[Content cut from Star Wars#Episode I The Phantom Menace|deleted scene]]}}</ref> and later included as part of the bonus material on the [[Star Wars home video releases#2001|DVD]], would be the same Greedo seen in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''A New Hope'']], and that Wald's prediction would foreshadow Greedo's death at the hands of [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]. [[George Lucas]] also stated in the commentary for the deleted scene that this was the same Greedo that appeared in ''A New Hope''.<ref name="TPM DVD">[[Star Wars home video releases#2001|''The Phantom Menace'' DVD]]</ref> However, in order to keep the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] backstory developed for the ''A New Hope'' character intact, ''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' [[Retroactive continuity|retconned]] the Rodian who fought with Anakin to be Greedo the Elder.<ref name="NEGC" /> However, in 2010, following the ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' episode [[Sphere of Influence]], which also featured Greedo, it was re-retconned that the Greedo in ''The Phantom Menace''{{'s}} deleted scene is indeed the very same Greedo of ''A New Hope''.<ref name="Chee">{{SWBoards|url=message.jspa?messageID=17933823#17933823|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2010-10-04|time=10:33 AM}}</ref>
